Here are the top relevant X posts (sorted by semantic relevance) matching your query on AI evaluation, benchmarking, adversarial testing, jailbreak detection, and AI safety testing. These focus on recent discussions around AI agent security benchmarks, jailbreak techniques, diagnostic frameworks, and cybersecurity evaluations.[[1]](https://x.com/i/status/2017550335049396639)[[2]](https://x.com/i/status/2018077848335249476)[[3]](https://x.com/i/status/2018967278193439131)

### Key Highlights:
- **AI Agent Security Benchmarks**: Building open benchmarks like MoltRank for testing AI agents against attack simulations.[[2]](https://x.com/i/status/2018077848335249476) Also, new research on AI agents in dynamic attack/defense CTF scenarios at AAAI26.[[4]](https://x.com/i/status/2019121944293949513)
- **Jailbreak & Adversarial Techniques**: A new "Semantic Chaining" method bypasses LLM guardrails by breaking malicious requests into benign steps.[[5]](https://x.com/i/status/2017460021630525936) Another simple jailbreak exploits AI's self-assessment of jailbreak risk.[[6]](https://x.com/i/status/2018153690289373561)
- **Diagnostic & Safety Frameworks**: "AgentDoG" – a new SOTA guardrail framework diagnosing AI agent issues like prompt injection with 82% accuracy.[[7]](https://x.com/i/status/2018980996528685464) Discussions on runtime safety infrastructure beyond pre-deployment evals.[[3]](https://x.com/i/status/2018967278193439131)
- **Broader Testing**: Emphasis on adversarial ML evaluation, red teaming, and penetration testing for security standards.[[8]](https://x.com/i/status/2019078676915384779) Enterprise uses for adversarial testing and scalable data labeling.[[9]](https://x.com/i/status/2018605687413133486)

Note: An exact keyword search returned no results (possibly due to specificity), but semantic search surfaced these high-relevance English-language posts from the past week. If you'd like more details, threads, images/videos, or refined searches, let me know!
